title: Dry Braised Malaysian Chestnut Satay
description: A nutty and crispy Malaysian dish featuring chestnuts, this recipe is a quick fix for a delicious meal, and is friendly to those with nut allergies. It is cooked on a wooden board for a unique presentation.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, coriander, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sliced chestnuts to the bowl and toss to coat with the soy sauce mixture.
Heat the vegetable oil on the wooden board over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the oil and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chestnuts to the wooden board and cook for 5 minutes on each side.
Transfer the wooden board to the preheated oven and bake for 10 minutes.
Remove the chestnuts from the oven and let cool for 2 minutes.
Serve the chestnuts hot, garnished with chopped scallions if desired.
